items.title: RBI launches the July 2026 round of the Inflation Expectations Survey of Households
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has been regularly conducting Inflation Expectations Survey of Households (IESH). The July 2026 round of the survey is being launched. The survey aims at capturing subjective assessments on price movements and inflation, based on their individual consumption baskets, across 19 cities, viz., Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Bhopal, Bhubaneswar, Chandigarh, Chennai, Delhi, Guwahati, Hyderabad, Jaipur, Jammu, Kolkata, Lucknow, Mumbai, Nagpur, Patna, Raipur, Ranchi and Thiruvananthapuram. The survey seeks qualitative responses from households on price changes (general prices as well

items.title: Kusumgar IPO: Issue booked 136 times on Day 03; NII, QIB portions see huge demand, GMP signals 39% premium
items.summary: Kusumgar Limited's IPO was oversubscribed by 136 times, with strong demand from all investor categories. The offer, comprising 1.55 crore equity shares, aims to enhance brand visibility without proceeds going to the company. Listing is expected on July 15, with a grey market premium of ₹163.

items.title: Q1 Results Today Live: Indian Bank, Bank of Maharashtra, Elecon Engineering post gains; Waaree Renewable Technologies, GK Energy, Yash Highvoltage to announce Q1 results
items.summary: Q1 results today live: Track all the latest Q1 FY27 earnings announcements, corporate developments and management commentary as companies including Elecon Engineering Company, Waaree Renewable Technologies, GK Energy and Yash Highvoltage report their June quarter results.

items.title: TCS shares close 1% higher after Q1 earnings beat; valuation zooms ₹7,706 cr
items.summary: Other major IT stocks such as Infosys, HCL Tech, Tech Mahindra and Wipro also end in positive territory

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures slip after tech-led rally; SK Hynix debut in focus
items.summary: US stock futures dipped on 10 July after a prior rally, but are poised for weekly gains amid easing Middle East tensions and falling oil prices. Investors await SK Hynix's ADR debut as chip stocks lead Wall Street's rise ahead of the approaching earnings season.

items.title: Sebi approves IPOs of defence electronics maker Tonbo Imaging and 3 others
items.summary: Sebi approved IPO proposals of Tonbo Imaging India, Marri Retail, Zetwerk and Gujarat Victory Forgings, strengthening the primary market pipeline. The companies plan fundraising for expansion, debt repayment and growth, with defence, retail, manufacturing and metals sectors represented.

items.title: SK Hynix makes US debut today. Will investors sell Micron to buy the AI memory leader?
items.summary: SK Hynix begins US trading after a $26.5 billion ADR sale, expanding investor access to its AI memory chip business. The Nasdaq listing could narrow its valuation gap with Micron, while testing investor appetite for AI-linked semiconductor stocks.

items.title: Chip giant SK Hynix raises $26.5bn in mega US share sale
items.summary: The shares are set to start trading on the Nasdaq on Friday in what will be the largest ever debut by a foreign firm.

items.title: India's non-fossil fuel-based energy capacity rises 22% to 297.36 GW in June
items.summary: India's non-fossil fuel energy capacity reached 297.36 GW in June. This represents a significant year-on-year increase of over twenty-two percent. Solar energy capacity grew substantially to 162.15 GW from 116.25 GW. Wind energy also saw an increase, contributing to the overall clean energy expansion. The nation continues its accelerated progress towards ambitious clean energy goals.

items.title: Man 'nearly sucked out of window mid-air' on Ryanair plane, passengers say
items.summary: Ryanair confirm a passenger was given medical treatment after an incident on a Malta Air flight, which is owned by Ryanair.

items.title: Quote of the day by Hetty Green: "There is no great secret in fortune making. All you have to do is to buy cheap and sell dear, act with thrift and shrewdness and then be persistent"
items.summary: Hetty Green's century-old advice on wealth creation remains relevant today. Investors should buy undervalued assets and sell them at higher prices. Practicing financial prudence and managing expenses wisely builds lasting wealth. Persistence through market volatility is a key investment strategy. These disciplined principles form the foundation for sustainable wealth creation.

items.title: Nifty reclaims 24,200; Midcap index hits record high
items.summary: Broader markets outperformed, with the Nifty Midcap 100 hitting a record high and the Nifty Smallcap 100 posting solid gains.

items.title: Market wrap: Top gainers and losers on Nifty and Sensex today
items.summary: Sensex climbed 828 points and Nifty gained 244 points, extending their winning streak as TCS earnings, positive global cues and banking optimism lifted sentiment. The rally added nearly Rs 6 lakh crore to BSE market capitalisation, with IT, financials and metals leading gains.

items.title: Distraction free IRCTC website in few months: Indian Railways
items.summary: Indian Railways is revamping its IRCTC website with a cleaner interface. The new portal will offer faster booking and easier repeat reservations. Seat availability will be visible across all train classes. The passenger reservation engine is also undergoing a significant upgrade. These improvements are expected to be available in the coming months.

items.title: Life insurance new business premiums rise 16.6% in Q1 on private insurers' growth: Report
items.summary: India's life insurance sector began FY27 with robust new business premiums, growing 16.6 percent. Private insurers significantly outpaced the market, increasing their share to nearly forty percent. This growth stemmed from steady retail product demand and a recovery in group business. Monthly premiums also saw a thirteen percent rise in June, reversing last year's contraction.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | S&P 500 | US Stock Market Today | Live: US stock futures mixed ahead of SK Hynix listing; Middle East in focus
Aluminium body urges Finance and Mines ministries to prevent India being made a scrap ‘dumping ground’
items.summary: It can have wider ramifications, affecting production quality while leading to a glut in domestic production, says the association

items.title: TRAI bars apps from blocking, tagging calls from 1600 number series
items.summary: Telecom regulator Trai stated no app can block calls from 1600 number series. Promotional calls from 140 number series also cannot be filtered by apps. These calls can only be blocked through the regulator's Do Not Disturb registry. This clarification means users cannot tag these specific calls as spam. Customers can register their preferences on the DND registry for blocking.

items.title: IEA warns of petrol and diesel supply crunch
items.summary: Refineries in Gulf and Russia hit by impact of wars, while global consumption has remained high

items.title: EU threatens Meta with fines over 'addictive' Facebook and Instagram
items.summary: Regulators say features such as infinite scroll contribute to "compulsive use" and "unhealthy habits".

items.title: India's 10-year bond logs best day in over a week on oil relief
items.summary: The 6.94% 2036 bond yield fell 3.8 basis points to 6.7139%, sliding the most in a day since July 2. ‌It ended little changed week-on-week

items.title: JioStar, TNT Sports, Willow and Cricbuzz secure broadcast rights for European T20 Premier League
items.summary: Europe's first ICC-sanctioned T20 league has secured broadcast agreements with JioStar and other partners. The inaugural season will be held from August 26 to September 20 across multiple continents. JioStar will televise and stream the tournament for Indian viewers on its platforms.

items.title: Freight woes threaten fresh supply crunch in Manipur as transporters stay away
items.summary: Transport operators are avoiding Manipur due to high costs and long travel times. Essential goods face shortages as highway conditions worsen significantly. Freight charges have increased substantially, impacting commodity prices. Retail prices for onions, potatoes, and edible oil have risen sharply. Further disruptions risk shortages, especially during the monsoon season.

items.title: India's forex reserves rise $7.26 billion to $674.19 billion in week ended July 3
items.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a substantial rise of $7.26 billion. These reserves reached $674.19 billion in the week ending July 326, 2026. The Reserve Bank of India closely monitors foreign exchange market developments. The RBI intervenes when necessary to ensure orderly market conditions. The central bank refrains from targeting any specific exchange rate.

items.title: Meta found to breach EU laws with 'addictive' Instagram, Facebook designs
items.summary: Instagram and Facebook's "addictive" designs have put Meta in breach of the European Union's digital laws, the EU concluded Friday in a preliminary report.

items.title: Expert view: Recent correction has provided ample opportunities in large caps, says DSP MF's market strategist
items.summary: In a recent interview, Sahil Kapoor of DSP Mutual Fund shares insights on leveraging current market corrections to invest in large-cap equities. Kapoor highlights sectoral opportunities like private banks and healthcare.

items.title: TCS shifts to outcome-based AI deals, phases savings for clients
items.summary: India's IT bellwether says it is generating up to 15% AI-led cost savings and passing the productivity gains to clients in phases.

items.title: Mumbai Metro One restructures debt with NARCL, avoids insolvency
items.summary: Mumbai Metro One Private Limited has successfully restructured its debt, avoiding insolvency. The company signed a Master Restructuring Agreement with NARCL for Rs 2,771.32 crore. This agreement strengthens the financial position of the metro operator. NARCL will now nominate a director to the company's board. A monitoring committee will oversee the restructuring implementation process.

items.title: Nykaa among 6 midcap stocks that hit 52-week highs, rally up to 30% in a month
items.summary: Nykaa, Godrej Industries and four other BSE MidCap stocks hit fresh 52-week highs, rallying up to 30% over the past month amid strong market momentum.

items.title: Expert view: Indian stock market may remain range-bound this year
items.summary: As foreign portfolio investors sell, domestic institutional investors rally, keeping the Indian stock market stable. Nikhil Chawla, managing partner and co-founder of xMultiplied Capital Advisors, however, warns that domestic support cannot fully replace foreign capital.

items.title: Aastha Spintex to acquire Falcon Yarns to expand manufacturing capacity
items.summary: Aastha Spintex has acquired Falcon Yarns to significantly expand its manufacturing capacity. This strategic acquisition will increase the company's spinning capacity to over seventeen thousand metric tons. The combined entity will possess a spindle capacity exceeding sixty-one thousand spindles. This expansion aims to meet rising customer demand and improve operational efficiencies. The deal strengthens Aastha Spintex's position in the cotton yarn industry.

items.title: Shapoorji Pallonji prices Rs 21, 500 cr financing with Deutsche, BlackRock among investors
items.summary: Shapoorji Pallonji Group secured significant funding through debt issuance. This refinancing effort attracted both domestic and global institutional investors. The company raised approximately Rs 21,500 crore in its initial funding tranche. Investors showed strong interest, with many participating for the first time. This move is part of a larger plan to replace higher cost debt.

items.title: Aukera raises ₹90 crore debt funding to expand lab-grown diamond jewellery business
items.summary: In a significant move, lab-grown diamond brand Aukera raised ₹90 crore in debt financing, spearheaded by Alteria Capital. This influx of capital is earmarked for enhancing retail presence and advancing product innovation. Aukera's strategy includes opening additional outlets in both familiar and new cities, as the company targets the upscale segment of the booming market, ensuring a robust balance sheet for upcoming industry shifts.

items.title: India's EV components opportunity to hit Rs 3.55 lakh crore by 2032 amid localisation push
items.summary: India's electric vehicle components market is projected to grow significantly. This market is expected to reach approximately Rs 3.55 lakh crore by 2032. Battery packs will dominate over half of this component market. However, battery packs and inverters remain import-dependent segments. Strategic investments will secure India's global EV value chain position.

items.title: The Tech Download: Teen social media bans miss a key part of the puzzle: AI chatbots
items.summary: Teenagers are increasingly becoming dependent on AI chatbots, echoing a familiar problem with social media in the 2010s.

items.title: IndiGo gets DGCA warning over cargo spillage, dangerous goods rule violations
items.summary: IndiGo's parent company received a DGCA warning letter for procedural deviations. This followed an audit after a cargo spillage incident occurred in January. The airline must now submit an action taken report to the aviation regulator. No penalties or sanctions were imposed by the DGCA on the airline. The company cited internal communication gaps for a delayed disclosure of this event.

items.title: Global PE firm Invus nears ₹500 crore minority stake in restaurant operator Popo Ventures
items.summary: Invus Group is close to investing ₹500 crore in Bengaluru-based restaurant operator Popo Ventures, underscoring rising private equity interest in India's fast-growing dining sector.

items.title: Passive flows -> active woes?
items.summary: A hot new active vs passive paper just dropped

items.title: Volkswagen to slash model lineup and shrink capacity — but no word on job cuts
items.summary: The update followed a high-stakes boardroom showdown with the group's supervisory board.

items.title: Ukraine escalates attacks on tankers near Crimea as Russian fuel shortages bite
items.summary: The drone strikes form part of Ukraine's campaign designed to choke off supplies and transportation routes in and out of Crimea.

items.title: Policybazaar ropes in Amitabh Bachchan for insurance awareness movement
items.summary: Policybazaar has appointed veteran actor Amitabh Bachchan as its brand ambassador. This move initiates a significant movement for health and term insurance awareness nationwide. The initiative aligns with IRDAI's vision of providing insurance for all Indians by 2047. Bachchan's established image of trust will encourage families to prioritize financial protection. This campaign aims to secure the future of over 140 crore Indians.

items.title: OpenAI exec Fidji Simo says she's stepping down due to chronic illness, will transition to advisor
items.summary: Fidji Simo stepped away from OpenAI in April for a medical leave, says she now has to focus on recovery.

items.title: HCG uses AI, patient data to personalise cancer treatment: BS Ajaikumar, founder
items.summary: Healthcare Global Enterprises is using artificial intelligence and patient data. Algorithms are being developed to personalise cancer treatment and improve outcomes. The company analyses clinical and genomic data to classify patients for better decisions. HCG partnered with Accenture to enhance its AI research capabilities.

items.title: Milky Mist said to launch $213 million IPO this month
items.summary: Milky Mist Dairy Food Ltd. is preparing for its large initial public offering. The company aims to launch the share sale as early as July or August. This offering is expected to be India's largest dairy sector IPO. Proceeds will fund manufacturing expansion and debt repayment. The company has completed investor roadshows for the upcoming launch.

items.title: The billionaire dreaming of AI data centres in the desert
items.summary: Servers should be powered by off-grid renewables, argues Envision founder Zhang Lei

items.title: Milky Mist eyes ₹2,035-crore IPO by July-end
items.summary: The proposed offering comprises a fresh issue of shares worth as much as ₹1,785 crore and an offer for sale of up to ₹250 crore by founders, according to the draft prospectus.

items.title: RBI is right to act on digital payment fraud. But some safeguards need sharper design
items.summary: Cyber fraud complaints and losses are increasing significantly in India's digital payment system. The Reserve Bank of India has proposed new safeguards to address these growing concerns. A one-hour lag on large transactions aims to reduce financial losses from fraud. Additional authentication for vulnerable users and scrutiny of mule accounts are also suggested. Customer-induced controls and a kill switch offer users more transaction management capabilities.

items.title: Swiggy resolves FSSAI licence issue linked to subsidiary Toing; says no food safety risk
items.summary: Swiggy said it has resolved an FSSAI licence-related issue concerning its food ordering and delivery platform Toing after updating licence particulars. The company clarified the matter involved no food safety concerns, received a modified FSSAI licence on July 9, and said no monetary penalty has been imposed or major financial impact is expected.

items.title: The return to flying faster than sound will start small
items.summary: The FAA's proposal to lift the decades-old ban on supersonic flights over land could revive civilian faster-than-sound travel. Powered by quieter boom technology demonstrated by NASA's X-59, the first commercial opportunity is expected to be high-end private jets, with airliners remaining a longer-term prospect due to cost, technology and regulatory hurdles.

items.title: Are the markets witnessing ‘AI fatigue’? Jefferies says US tech earnings to test investment cycle
items.summary: Report says investors now focused on quarterly results of four major US hyperscalers - Alphabet, Amazon, Meta and Microsoft

items.title: Multibaggers: 13 stocks surged up to 225% in just 3 months
items.summary: Even as the Sensex stayed nearly flat with just a 0.14% gain over three months, 13 stocks with a market cap above Rs 5,000 crore bucked the trend, surging up to 225% and more than doubling investor wealth in the same period.

items.title: Rupee slips on week as Iran flare-up spurs caution, merchant hedging
items.summary: The Indian rupee ended nearly unchanged on Friday, but the week closed on a weaker note. Investor caution grew amid renewed hostilities in the Middle East, impacting oil-affiliated currencies. Meanwhile, global stock markets thrived, propelled by AI innovation and SK Hynix's entry in the US. The Japanese yen gained momentum due to pension funds' activities, and heightened hedging by importers swayed the rupee's demand among merchants.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Markets end in green; Sensex gains 827 pts, Nifty closes at 24,207
items.summary: Stock Market Highlights: Sensex ended 827.57 points (+1.08%) up at 77,569.39, while Nifty 50 closed 244.10 points (1.02%) up at 24,206.90

items.title: Monsoon revival lifts kharif hopes, but experts say El Niño threat is far from over
items.summary: India’s southwest monsoon has bounced back after a weak June, cutting the rainfall deficit and boosting kharif prospects. Experts caution, however, that the El Nino risk isn’t over.

items.title: Bank of Maharashtra Q1 results: Net profit jumps 27% YoY to ₹2,020 crore
items.summary: Bank of Maharashtra's net profit rose 27% to ₹2,020 crore in Q1 FY27, driven by growth in interest income and improved asset quality. Total income increased to ₹9,063 crore, and the bank's GNPA ratio improved to 1.45%.

items.title: Salasar Techno Engineering aims 20% revenue growth in FY27 to Rs 1,800 crore
items.summary: Salasar Techno Engineering targets twenty percent revenue growth this fiscal year. The company expects its revenue to reach approximately Rs 1,800 crore. This growth is supported by a substantial order book of about Rs 2,500 crore. Strong execution momentum is evident across key business verticals.

items.title: Smallcap company announces 1:10 stock split 7 days after listing on market
items.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism approved a 1:10 stock split, subject to shareholder approval, days after its market debut. The move aims to improve stock liquidity, affordability and retail participation without altering market capitalisation or intrinsic value. Record date is pending.

items.title: Abivax $920 million cash haul lets it prepare for solo U.S. launch instead of relying on a buyer, CEO tells CNBC
items.summary: Its latest fundraising round provides the French biotech with enough cash to finance operations through 2029.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 10 July: Godrej Industries, Indian Bank, Kalyan Jewellers, Paytm, CDSL among top gainers
items.summary: Domestic equity markets saw gains on July 10, supported by financial stocks and IT giant TCS, despite closing the week with slight losses. Major indices rose over 1%, led by Nifty Realty's 3.40% surge, while IT stocks benefited from TCS's strong earnings.

items.title: Finance ministry calls lenders for meeting on 13 July to review, step up foreign currency mobilization
items.summary: The government wants to boost the inflow of funds through foreign currency non-resident (bank) deposits, overseas foreign currency borrowings and external commercial borrowings.

items.title: Elon Musk stuns with a U-turn on Anthropic, calls it ‘the leader in AI’ after years of public spat
items.summary: Elon Musk has reversed his stance on Anthropic, praising its advancements in AI and acknowledging he was wrong about the startup. He now considers Anthropic the leader in AI.

items.title: AMFI June data: Equity mutual fund inflows jump 26%, mid-cap, small-cap funds steal the show
items.summary: Investors demonstrated strong interest in equity mutual funds in June, with net inflows reaching ₹28,973 crore, up 26% from May. Mid-cap and small-cap funds led inflows, illustrating sustained confidence in India's long-term growth despite market volatility and global challenges.

items.title: DU UG admissions 2026: Over 1.9 lakh register, first seat list on July 16
items.summary: Delhi University received 1.9 lakh applications for undergraduate admissions this year. Over 1.64 lakh candidates completed preference filling for programs and colleges. A correction window is open until Saturday evening for applicant data errors. The university offers 73 undergraduate programs with 71,624 seats available. The first seat allocation list will be released on July 16.

items.title: World Cup streaming giant draws scrutiny for promoting gambling
items.summary: Brazilian regulators are looking into CazéTV's aggressive betting promotions during FIFA World Cup broadcasts. The popular YouTube channel faces new government restrictions on advertising to young viewers. This controversy has brought to the fore concerns about gambling's influence on sports and youth.

items.title: Nuvama initiates Buy call on Vedanta Aluminium shares, expects profitability to exceed historical average. Here's why
items.summary: Nuvama Institutional Equities and Motilal Oswal Financial Services have initiated coverage on Vedanta Aluminium with 'Buy' ratings and target prices of Rs 540, citing strong earnings growth, cost reduction initiatives, firm aluminium prices and robust cash flow generation as key drivers.

items.title: Groww says direct mutual fund plans remain unchanged as it rolls out MF Prime
items.summary: Groww clarifies MF Prime is designed for investors who want guidance on selecting, reviewing and rebalancing mutual fund portfolios

items.title: National Medical Commission directs 70 colleges to comply with mandatory CCTV surveillance norms
items.summary: Seventy medical colleges must now connect their NVRs to the National Medical Commission. These institutions failed to comply with mandatory CCTV surveillance regulations. The commission issued a public notice after repeated follow-ups proved unsuccessful. Rajasthan, Maharashtra, and Delhi have the highest number of defaulting institutions. Non-compliant colleges must implement requirements without delay and seek assistance if needed.

items.title: SEBI cancels registration of 12 research analysts for non-payment of renewal fee
items.summary: The entities whose registrations have been cancelled include Arjun Lenin, CNI Research Ltd, East Bridge Advisors Pvt Ltd, Kushank Kamal Poddar and R K Global Shares & Securities Ltd

items.title: Lord's Mark Industries Limited listed on the Bombay Stock Exchange
items.summary: Lord's Mark Industries Limited successfully listed on the Bombay Stock Exchange on Friday, July 10. This significant milestone marks a new phase in the company's corporate journey. The diversified business group operates across energy, IVD, and MedTech sectors. The listing reflects a commitment to transparency and sustainable growth for stakeholders. The company aims to enhance market presence and create value across its verticals.

items.title: Indian Railways approves Rs 175 crore project for additional electric locomotive homing facilities at Raipur
items.summary: Indian Railways has approved a Rs 175 crore project for new locomotive facilities. These new facilities will accommodate 250 three-phase electric locomotives at Raipur. The project aims to strengthen maintenance infrastructure for expanding operations. This initiative will enhance the utilization of existing railway infrastructure. It also creates space for future technological advancements at the depot.

items.title: Sensex jumps 800 points, Nifty 50 reclaims 24,200; investors earn ₹6 lakh crore in a day: What drove the stock market?
items.summary: The Sensex jumped 828 points, or 1.08%, to end at 77,569.39, while the Nifty 50 settled at 24,206.90, up 244 points, or 1.02%. Investors earned nearly ₹6 lakh crore in a single session.

items.title: Renault India joins hands with IIT Kanpur for vehicle R&D
items.summary: Renault India and IIT Kanpur have formed a strategic research partnership. The collaboration will utilize IIT Kanpur's wind tunnel facility for vehicle testing. This initiative aims to enhance Renault's engineering capabilities within India. Advanced aerodynamic and wind-noise testing will be conducted on passenger vehicles. The agreement establishes a framework for commercial engagement and intellectual property.

items.title: Vapes to have less enticing names and flavours to protect children
items.summary: People are being consulted about plans to stop vape companies using enticing flavour descriptions that "attract" children into experimenting.

items.title: NSE to pitch IPO to 30 global investors, betting big on India’s deepening capital markets
items.summary: NSE projects double-digit growth across equities, derivatives and bond markets over the next five years

items.title: HCL in 'positive direction' on takeover of 4 copper blocks in Chile; advisors studying data
items.summary: Hindustan Copper Limited is advancing positively on acquiring four Chilean copper blocks. Transaction advisors are studying data and regulatory issues are being examined. This move aims to secure critical raw materials for India's green energy transition. The company is bidding jointly with NTPC Mining and Coal India Limited.

items.title: Bengaluru, Mumbai, Delhi NCR: A tale of three housing markets
items.summary: Bengaluru's housing market shows strong growth fueled by its expanding technology sector. Mumbai remains India's largest residential market, absorbing new supply effectively. Delhi-NCR faces a slowdown as premium homes dominate its available inventory. These contrasting trends indicate future property cycles will depend on job and investment growth. Infrastructure development is increasingly shaping buyer decisions across these key cities.

items.title: EasyJet agrees to surprise takeover bid as rival US firm swoops in
items.summary: The airline says a bid from US firm Apollo has trumped a recent takeover offer from Castlelake.

items.title: Indian Railways approves Rs 206 crore Kavach deployment on 680 route km of Northern Railway
items.summary: Indian Railways approved Kavach Version 4.0 deployment on 680 route kilometers. This automatic train protection system will be installed on Rewari-Delhi and Shakurbasti-Bathinda sections. The project, costing Rs 206 crore, aims to enhance train safety and operational efficiency. Kavach prevents signal passing incidents and train collisions, ensuring reliable operations. Deployment will support faster movement of passenger and freight trains across these important routes.

items.title: Why 1 in 3 Indian women have never made a solo financial decision, and why that's challenging
items.summary: Despite rising incomes and careers, many Indian women still don't make independent financial decisions. Financial literacy, confidence and structured learning are helping more women take control of their wealth and future.

items.title: Vivo-Dixon deal approval reveals India's new China playbook

items.title: Cotton sowing picks up in July as monsoon revives, still below last year
items.summary: Cotton sowing has accelerated in July as the southwest monsoon strengthens across India. Acreage has narrowed the gap compared to last year after initial delays. Improved rains in states like Maharashtra and Gujarat are encouraging farmers to plant cotton. Some farmers are shifting from paddy to cotton due to better price prospects. Overall kharif sowing is progressing well, with July being a crucial month for planting.

items.title: Soybean sowing gains pace, acreage seen surpassing last year
items.summary: Soybean sowing has accelerated across key Indian states after a monsoon delay. Nearly ninety percent of targeted areas in Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan are covered. Maharashtra has completed eighty to ninety percent of its soybean planting. The Soybean Processors Association of India expects total acreage to exceed last year. Favorable rainfall will determine the final soybean production outcome this season.

This decision affects corporate restructuring and group reorganizations. Companies must now re-evaluate demerger structures and their tax implications. The ruling clarifies statutory conditions for tax-neutral demergers under the Income-tax Act.

items.title: India's richer middle class is living a more dollar-linked life while earning in rupees
items.summary: Dollar spending India: The country's expanding middle class now spends more on foreign goods and services. International travel and overseas education are becoming regular annual expenses for many families. Digital subscriptions, AI tools, and imported electronics also contribute to this growing dollar demand. While oil imports remain the largest driver, household spending is a significant factor. This trend reflects India's deeper integration with the global economy.

Both nations committed to deepening bilateral energy trade and strengthening supply chains. They will advance energy trade and investment through existing economic agreements. items.title: India consumer inflation likely breached RBI's 4% target in June, poll shows
items.summary: India's consumer inflation likely breached the central bank's target in June. Higher food and fuel prices added to cost pressures for consumers. Economists forecast inflation quickened to 4.3% from May's 3.93%. Wholesale price inflation eased marginally while core inflation remained steady. This marks the highest inflation reading since India revised its consumer price index.
